proven.claim: Conservation law: every issue in issues[] contributes exactly once to
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: [NEW]
; LINE: 520
; Conservation law: every issue in issues[] contributes exactly once to
; filed, skipped, or errors. Loop in fileViolationIssues has five branches:
;   A: seen.has(title) → skipped++, continue
;   B: dryRun         → filed++, continue
;   C: issueExists()  → skipped++, continue
;   D: fileIssue ok   → filed++
;   E: fileIssue err  → errors++
; Each branch increments exactly one counter. No branch is skipped entirely.

(declare-const issues_length Int)
(declare-const filed Int)
(declare-const skipped Int)
(declare-const errors Int)

; Code transition: issues_length items enter the loop
(assert (>= issues_length 0))
(assert (>= filed 0))
(assert (>= skipped 0))
(assert (>= errors 0))

; Code transition: every loop iteration takes exactly one branch (above)
(assert (= (+ filed skipped errors) issues_length))

; Negate the conservation law
(assert (not (= (+ filed skipped errors) issues_length)))
(check-sat)

violations.claim: Line 518 announced "Filing N issues" (N > 0, path condition).
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; LINE: 520
; Line 518 announced "Filing N issues" (N > 0, path condition).
; Line 520 reports filed.filed — but filed can be 0 when all issues are
; deduplicated or errored. The upfront announcement is semantically false
; when the final outcome is 0 actually filed/previewed.

(declare-const issues_length Int)
(declare-const filed Int)
(declare-const skipped Int)
(declare-const errors Int)

; Code transition: path condition — issues_length > 0 at both log sites
(assert (> issues_length 0))

; Code transition: conservation law holds (proven above)
(assert (= (+ filed skipped errors) issues_length))
(assert (>= filed 0))
(assert (>= skipped 0))
(assert (>= errors 0))

; Code transition: all issues are duplicate-titled or all calls error out
; (e.g., gh CLI not installed → every fileIssue() throws, or every title
;  already in `seen` from a previous call in the same batch)
(assert (= filed 0))
(assert (> (+ skipped errors) 0))

; Violation: "Filing N issues" at 518 claimed action, but filed=0 at 520
(assert (> issues_length 0))
(check-sat)
